revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:地神五代]][[zh:地神五代]]
'''Chijin Godai''' (地神五代) refers to the five deities of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|火折尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, and {{ill|Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373935}} in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. It also denotes the era associated with these deities, corresponding to the "Lower Age of the Gods" (神代下) in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}''. These deities are positioned between the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神代七代|Heavenly Generations (Tenjin Shichidai)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神世七代}} and the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|qid=Q188210|lt=List of emperors of Japan|1=List of emperors of Japan}}.
The term "Chijin" (地神) means "earthly gods," contrasting with "Tenjin" (天神), meaning "heavenly gods."
While the ''Nihon Shoki'' of the 8th century does not explicitly use the terms "Tenjin Shichidai" or "Chijin Godai," by the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, these classifications had already been formalized.<ref>[http://www1.tcue.ac.jp/home1/k-gakkai/ronsyuu/ronsyuukeisai/49_3.4/nito.pdf "The Genealogy of Sovereigns in the Nihon Shoki"] by Kyo Nito, ''Takasaki University of Economics Journal'', Vol. 49, Nos. 3-4, 2007.</ref>
Among the Chijin Godai, the three deities {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|火折尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, and {{ill|Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373935}} are also collectively referred to as the "Three Generations of Hyuga" ({{ill|Hyuga Sandai|ja|日向三代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyuga Sandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Three generations of Hyuga|qid=Q56347075}}) in the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' (compiled in the 9th century).
== Genealogy == {{Imperial Family Chijin Godai}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial ancestor gods|ja|皇祖神|1=Ancestors of the Emperor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ancestor gods|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestors of the Emperor|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanja-sanryō|ja|神代三陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanja-sanryō|1=Kamiyo Sanryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28683511}}
